evidence: It's a glorious yet calm look into the lives of girls who are cyborg assassins.
It's a wonderful film, high in its characterizations and interactions.
The first two episodes are hard to follow as they jump back and forth between scenes happening at different times.
The story starts to take twists as murders pop up, a mysterious figure keeps showing itself and flashings between fantasy and the real world become more frequent.
It is about their present lives, how they are handling it, how real they actually are and what kind of lives they live.
It's a short film, nothing grand and drawn out.
The animation is superb and the blend with CG and animation is very seamless and very well executed.
The art style, realistic and very beautiful, a worthy Masterpiece of art.

I'm a very open-minded person. When it comes to Anime, I pretty much look at everything from an objective point of view, without ties into what I wanted it to be... but with Kenshin it's a little different.
Armitage the Third was one of my more favorite Anime's.
It is a movie about emotions, and they can be heavy at times.
The moments of silence reminded me of that uncomfortable moment in Evangelion in the elevator.
I liked the anime. I thought it was very different from what I originally perceived it to be, and so it was a nice surprise later on as I got to understand what it was about.
It's a wonderful film, high in its characterizations and interactions.

summary: The user has a strong preference for complex narratives and character-driven stories across various genres, particularly drama and sci-fi. They appreciate high production quality and emotional depth in their viewing experiences, often favoring slower-paced, introspective content over action-heavy plots.
